frame_analyses.description: Title slide for FLS instructional video on Tasks 4 & 5
Setup view showing laparoscopic instruments positioned over Penrose drain with black marks
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task setup complete, instruments in starting position

frame_analyses.description: Needle being guided through first mark on Penrose drain using needle drivers
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Precise needle placement through black mark, good instrument control

frame_analyses.description: Suture passes through both marks on the Penrose drain, showing completed suture placement
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both suture passes completed accurately through target marks

frame_analyses.description: Beginning formation of surgeon's knot (double throw) for first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Initiating double throw technique for surgeon's knot

frame_analyses.description: Completing surgeon's knot formation with needle drivers
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Double throw being secured, appears to be proper surgeon's knot technique

frame_analyses.description: Forming second throw with single loop, hands appear switched from first throw
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Single throw formation, hand switching demonstrated correctly

frame_analyses.description: Final throw being formed and tightened, completing the knot sequence
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Third throw completion, hands switched again as required

frame_analyses.description: Cutting suture ends with laparoscopic scissors
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Task completion with suture cutting inside trainer

knot_assessments.notes: Proper surgeon's knot (double throw) technique demonstrated
Single throw with proper hand switching
Final single throw with hand switching, knot appears secure

drain_assessment.assessment_note: Drain appears well-approximated with no gaps visible

technique_summary: This is an instructional video demonstrating proper FLS Task 5 technique with perfect suture placement and knot tying form. All procedural steps are executed correctly.
improvement_suggestions: This is educational content - actual trainee performance would require timing assessment
Focus on replicating the demonstrated technique during practice
strengths: Perfect demonstration of surgeon's knot technique
Accurate suture placement on target marks
